definitions.anterior vertebral muscle: A muscle of neck that is a member of the prevertebral group situated between the prevertebral fascia and the cervical vertebral column, comprising the longus capitis, longus colli, rectus capitis anterior, and rectus capitis lateralis, innervated by cervical spinal nerve roots (C1-C8), and functioning primarily to flex and stabilize the head and neck at the craniocervical junction.
definitions.circular pharyngeal muscle: A muscle of neck that forms the outer circular layer of the pharyngeal musculature, comprising the superior, middle, and inferior constrictor muscles, which encircle the pharynx and function to constrict the pharyngeal lumen during swallowing to propel the food bolus into the esophagus; innervated chiefly by the pharyngeal branch of the vagus nerve (CN X).

possible_matches.note: UBERON:0008453 has 'anterior vertebral muscle' as a related synonym but refers to a single muscle (rectus capitis anterior), not the prevertebral muscle group. The NTR refers to the group-level concept (prevertebral muscles). Treat as possible_match only; the group-level term is absent from UBERON.
